fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity florida, how to transfer dietitian license to florida, dietitian compact florida, florida dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council
board.parent_dept: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine
board.physical_address:
4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C-05
Tallahassee, FL 32399-3255

quick_facts.license_duration: Up to 2 Years (expires May 31 odd years)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist
license_types.description: Florida issues a combined credential. Requires minimum bachelor's degree, 900 hours of supervised practice, and passing a licensing examination (typically the CDR).

license_types.title: Licensed Nutrition Counselor
license_types.description: An alternative credential path in Florida; requirements vary.

steps.title: Registration and Education
steps.description: Complete your degree and supervised practice. Most applicants use their active CDR registration as proof of passing a recognized examination.

steps.title: Apply Online
steps.description: Submit your application for Licensure by Endorsement or Examination through the Florida Department of Health MQA Services portal and pay the $165 total fee.

steps.title: Background Screening
steps.description: Undergo fingerprinting for a criminal history background check through a Livescan service provider.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from the educational institution.
Verification sent directly to the Board.

provisional_text: Florida offers a temporary permit for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the examination. It requires an additional fee and direct supervision.
reciprocity_text: Florida allows Licensure by Endorsement for professionals who hold a current, active license in another state with equivalent or more stringent requirements.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The Florida Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council charges a total initial fee of $165. This includes a $50 application fee, $110 license fee, and $5 unlicensed activity fee.

faqs.question: Is a background check required for a Florida dietitian license?
faqs.answer: Yes. All applicants must undergo a Livescan fingerprint background check as part of the application process with the Florida Department of Health.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Florida?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 30 days (initial review). If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Florida allows Licensure by Endorsement for professionals who hold a current, active license in another state with equivalent or more stringent requirements.
